title: artemisia gmelinii var. minor
intro: Artemisia gmelinii var. minor, also known as Russian tarragon, is a perennial plant species belonging to the asteraceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Russia and China. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oil, which contains compounds such as borneol, limonene, and camphor, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Russian tarragon include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, Russian tarragon is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used as a flavoring agent in cooking due to its anise-like flavor.
